title: Adopt IOptions pattern for configuration management
context: Injecting IConfiguration directly creates high coupling to configuration keys and structure. It makes unit testing difficult and bypasses the validation/binding capabilities of the Options pattern. This violates .NET configuration best practices.

title: Refactor App.xaml.cs to use Generic Host pattern
context: App.xaml.cs is acting as a composition root and process orchestrator, mixing UI logic (creating windows) with backend service management. This violates separation of concerns and makes the application hard to test and maintain. Modern WPF applications should use Generic Host pattern.

title: Adopt C# 12 primary constructors for cleaner code
context: Verbose boilerplate for constructor dependency injection. .NET 8 / C# 12 supports Primary Constructors to reduce verbosity and improve code readability. The project targets .NET 8 but doesn't leverage modern C# 12 features.

title: Fix unsafe async cleanup in application shutdown
context: Calling async methods with _ = inside OnExit (a void method) creates a race condition. The application process may terminate before the cleanup tasks complete, potentially leaving orphaned backend processes or incomplete cleanup.

title: Adopt C# 12 collection expressions for modern syntax
context: Project uses old collection initialization syntax (new List<>, new Dictionary<>) instead of C# 12 collection expressions. Modern syntax improves readability and is more concise. Found in multiple files including LibreHardwareManager.cs:100, DxgiGpuMonitor.cs:199.

title: Replace synchronous semaphore wait with async WaitAsync
context: Using _updateLock.Wait() (synchronous semaphore wait) in LibreHardwareManager.UpdateSnapshot() can cause thread pool starvation. Should use WaitAsync() for async-friendly synchronization.

title: Implement IAsyncDisposable for async resource cleanup
context: Classes with async cleanup operations (LibreHardwareManager, SystemMetricProvider) only implement IDisposable, not IAsyncDisposable. This forces blocking async operations in Dispose(), which is an anti-pattern. SignalRService correctly implements IAsyncDisposable.

title: Add logging to exception handlers instead of empty catch
context: Multiple empty catch blocks found that silently swallow exceptions without logging or handling. This makes debugging difficult and hides potential issues. Found in test files and tools, but pattern should be avoided.

title: Use configuration file for service executable location
context: App.xaml.cs contains hardcoded relative paths ('..\..\..\..\XhMonitor.Service') to locate the backend service. This couples the runtime application to a specific development directory structure, which will fail in different deployment environments (production, CI/CD, user installations).

title: Extract DXGI and Performance Counter logic into separate provider classes
context: SystemMetricProvider orchestrates high-level providers (Cpu/GpuProvider) but also contains low-level implementation details for DXGI monitoring and Performance Counters directly within the class. This mixing of abstraction levels violates the Single Level of Abstraction Principle and makes the class harder to understand and maintain.

title: Centralize port configuration with dynamic fallback
context: Ports 35179 (Service) and 35180 (Web) are hardcoded in multiple places across Desktop, Service, and configuration files. Port conflicts will cause the application to fail start without recourse. Changes require updating multiple files, increasing maintenance burden.

title: Use collection-based provider management for dynamic metrics
context: SystemMetricProvider defines specific fields for CPU, GPU, Memory, VRAM providers (_cpuProvider, _gpuProvider, etc.). This prevents dynamically adding new system-level metrics (e.g., Disk, Network) without changing the class structure. The design is not extensible for future metric types.

title: Use factory pattern or DI for provider instantiation
context: MetricProviderRegistry's RegisterBuiltInProviders method directly instantiates concrete provider classes (CpuMetricProvider, GpuMetricProvider, etc.) instead of using dependency injection or factory patterns. This creates tight coupling and makes it difficult to test or swap implementations.

title: Extract process management into IProcessManager interface
context: App.xaml.cs directly manages Process objects for Service and Web Server without any abstraction layer. This makes it difficult to test process lifecycle management and couples the application to specific process management implementation details.

title: Establish consistent error handling patterns for configuration
context: Configuration loading in SettingsViewModel uses try-catch with generic error handling, while Program.cs throws exceptions for missing connection strings. This inconsistency makes it difficult to understand error handling patterns and leads to unpredictable behavior.

title: Skip full process list refresh when floating panel is collapsed
context: Problem: the floating desktop panel still performs full process synchronization, sorting, and ObservableCollection updates even when the details popup is collapsed. Evidence: XhMonitor.Desktop/ViewModels/FloatingWindowViewModel.cs:409, :455, :468 and XhMonitor.Desktop/FloatingWindow.xaml:494. Impact: unnecessary UI-thread work continues in collapsed state, causing avoidable CPU usage and jitter. Scope: XhMonitor.Desktop floating window process refresh path only. Acceptance: when collapsed, the view model skips full AllProcesses/TopProcesses refresh and keeps only minimal state updates required for the collapsed bar and pinned items.

title: Virtualize floating process list and simplify row template
context: Problem: the floating process list uses ScrollViewer + ItemsControl + StackPanel without virtualization, and each row template is visually heavy with ToolTip, multiple ProgressBar controls, MultiBinding, and mouse interaction hooks. Evidence: XhMonitor.Desktop/FloatingWindow.xaml:81, :535, :608. Impact: opening the details popup with many processes creates all visual elements at once and can cause frame drops and input lag. Scope: XhMonitor.Desktop floating popup process list only. Acceptance: the process list uses a virtualized items control and the row visual tree is reduced enough to handle large process counts smoothly.

title: Avoid full process snapshot broadcast and full client recompute for floating/widget
context: Problem: the service broadcasts full process snapshots (ReceiveProcessMetrics) to all clients, while floating UI and web widget only need summary and a small top-N slice; meanwhile the web floating widget aggregates and sorts the entire process array on every push and uses unstable keys. Llama realtime loop can also enqueue extra full snapshots on small llama-only changes. Evidence: XhMonitor.Service/Worker.cs:628, :661, :408 and xhmonitor-web/src/pages/DesktopWidget/FloatingWidget.tsx:20, :26, :264 plus xhmonitor-web/src/utils.ts:67. Impact: serialization/transport/deserialization and client compute scale with total process count, and extra snapshot pushes amplify UI thread and render jitter. Scope: process metrics push path + floating/widget aggregation and rendering. Acceptance: floating/widget consumers can use lightweight payloads (server-side pre-aggregation/top-N or delta channel), clients avoid full-array recompute and use stable keys, and llama-only changes do not trigger unnecessary full snapshot broadcasts.

title: Throttle or cache TaskbarMetricsViewModel column rebuild work
context: Problem: the taskbar mini window rebuilds metric columns on every system usage and hardware limit update, including repeated FormattedText measurement and layout calculation. Evidence: XhMonitor.Desktop/ViewModels/TaskbarMetricsViewModel.cs:203, :241, :265, :895. Impact: repeated layout work can cause jitter on weak machines or higher refresh scenarios, even though SyncColumns reduces collection churn. Scope: TaskbarMetricsViewModel rebuild path only. Acceptance: unchanged measurements are reused or rebuild frequency is reduced so normal metric updates do not trigger full column reconstruction each time.

title: Reuse scrollbar hide timer and cache scrollbar lookup in floating popup
context: Problem: every scroll offset change recursively searches the visual tree for the ScrollBar and allocates a new DispatcherTimer to hide it later. Evidence: XhMonitor.Desktop/FloatingWindow.xaml.cs:936, :968. Impact: extra allocations and visual-tree traversal during scrolling add avoidable UI overhead and can amplify stutter. Scope: floating popup scroll behavior only. Acceptance: the ScrollBar reference is cached and a single reusable timer controls hide/show behavior.

title: Reduce overdraw from transparent windows and shadow effects in desktop widgets
context: Problem: both floating and taskbar windows use AllowsTransparency with shadow effects, which is likely to amplify composition and redraw cost in WPF. Evidence: XhMonitor.Desktop/FloatingWindow.xaml:11, :298, :498 and XhMonitor.Desktop/Windows/TaskbarMetricsWindow.xaml:13 plus XhMonitor.Desktop/Windows/TaskbarMetricsWindow.xaml.cs:476. Impact: drag, animation, and frequent metric updates may cost more GPU and UI-thread time than necessary. Scope: floating window, popup, and taskbar widget visual effects. Acceptance: expensive transparency/shadow combinations are reduced or isolated so the desktop widgets render with lower overdraw cost.

title: Deduplicate SignalR hub connections in main web app
context: Problem: the main web app establishes multiple SignalR hub connections (TimeSeriesContext/useTimeSeries + useMetricsHub) instead of sharing one connection. Evidence: xhmonitor-web/src/App.tsx:24, xhmonitor-web/src/contexts/TimeSeriesContext.tsx:12, xhmonitor-web/src/hooks/useTimeSeries.ts:50, xhmonitor-web/src/hooks/useMetricsHub.ts:30. Impact: redundant connections increase server/client overhead and can duplicate message handling. Scope: xhmonitor-web main app hub connection lifecycle only (not widget-only routes). Acceptance: the main web page uses a single shared hub connection and hooks reuse it without creating additional connections.

title: Investigate or remove unused metrics.latest SignalR subscription
context: Problem: desktop client subscribes to both ReceiveProcessMetrics and metrics.latest, but no sender for metrics.latest is found in the service code, while README/docs still mention it. Evidence: XhMonitor.Desktop/Services/SignalRService.cs:78, :106 and XhMonitor.Desktop/ViewModels/FloatingWindowViewModel.cs:308, :369 plus README.md:285. Impact: risk of duplicated process updates if both events are ever emitted, and extra maintenance confusion (stale docs / dead subscription). Scope: desktop SignalR subscriptions + service hub push surface + related docs. Acceptance: either implement a clear metrics.latest sender (and ensure only one processing path is active) or remove the subscription and update docs so clients rely on a single, well-defined process update channel.
